findings.title: Microsoft Defender Zero-Day Trilogy — RedSun + UnDefend Join BlueHammer in Active Exploitation
findings.summary: Two additional Microsoft Defender zero-days (RedSun LPE to SYSTEM, UnDefend definition update blocker) are now confirmed actively exploited alongside BlueHammer, with Huntress Labs observing hands-on-keyboard activity across all three — only BlueHammer is patched.

findings.title: CVE-2026-21962 — Oracle WebLogic Server Proxy Plug-in Unauthenticated RCE via HTTP (CVSS 10.0)
findings.affected_technology: Oracle HTTP Server / Oracle WebLogic Server Proxy Plug-in
findings.summary: An easily exploitable unauthenticated vulnerability in Oracle WebLogic Server Proxy Plug-in allows complete system compromise via HTTP with no user interaction required.

findings.title: Cursor AI Vulnerability Chain — Prompt Injection + Sandbox Bypass Enables Shell Access on Developer Machines
findings.summary: An indirect prompt injection in Cursor AI can be chained with a sandbox bypass and remote tunnel feature to obtain full shell access on developer machines, representing a critical AI coding assistant attack surface.

findings.title: Payouts King Ransomware — QEMU VM Evasion Bypasses All Host Endpoint Security via SonicWall VPN Entry
findings.affected_technology: Windows endpoints (SonicWall VPN, SolarWinds WHD initial access)
findings.summary: Payouts King ransomware operators deploy hidden QEMU Alpine Linux VMs with SYSTEM privileges to bypass all host-based EDR, entering via exposed SonicWall VPNs and SolarWinds WHD, linked to GOLD ENCOUNTER / former BlackBasta affiliates.

findings.title: CVE-2026-0933 — Cloudflare Wrangler CI/CD Command Injection via --commit-hash (CVSS 9.9)
findings.summary: A command injection flaw in Cloudflare Wrangler's pages deploy --commit-hash parameter enables arbitrary shell command execution in CI/CD pipelines where the parameter derives from untrusted sources.

findings.title: ClickFix Phishing Campaign Impersonating Claude AI Installer — Staged PowerShell + Process Injection
findings.summary: Rapid7 Labs identified a ClickFix phishing campaign using a fake Claude MSIX installer that delivers staged malware via mshta, AMSI bypass, XOR-encrypted shellcode, and process injection targeting EU and US developers.

findings.title: CVE-2026-40351 — FastGPT NoSQL Injection Authentication Bypass (CVSS 9.8)
findings.summary: A TypeScript type assertion without runtime validation in FastGPT allows MongoDB query operators in login credentials, bypassing authentication for administrator accounts on this open-source AI knowledge base platform.

findings.title: CVE-2026-32105 — xrdp Missing MAC Signature Verification Enables MITM on RDP Sessions (CVSS 9.3)
findings.summary: The xrdp remote desktop server silently ignores MAC signatures in classic RDP mode, allowing man-in-the-middle attackers to intercept and modify encrypted RDP session traffic without detection.

noted.summary: OS command injection via unsanitized configuration constants in shell exec() calls (CVSS 9.4, requires admin access).

noted.product: Anviz CX2 Lite / CX7 access control
noted.summary: Unauthenticated firmware upload enables attacker code execution and reverse shell on physical access control devices (CVSS 9.8).
Authentication check fails when API key is unset, exposing bot control and downstream integrations to unauthenticated access (CVSS 9.1).
FortiGuard reports new Mirai variant Nexcorium targeting TBK DVRs via CVE-2024-3721 with multi-architecture DDoS and brute-force capabilities.
Critical heap buffer overflows in PSD, TGA, and XWD codecs enable RCE via crafted image files (CVSS 9.8).
Backup restore functionality lacks file filtering, enabling PHP webshell upload and execution (CVSS 9.1).
ShinyHunters exfiltrated 13.5M user accounts from McGraw Hill's Salesforce environment; contact data now public, creating spear-phishing risk.
